DECLARATION OF YOSEF PERETZ IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFFS MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER INSPECTION OF PROPERTY AND FOR SANCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 61527754) FILED BY PLAINTIFF TERGIS, BENEZRA ZAWITZ, AMIEE ROSE

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS BENEZRA TERGIS, AMIEE ZAWITZ'S MOTION TO COMPEL INSPECTION OF PROPERTY AND FOR SANCTIONS. COUNSEL STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHALL MAKE THE PROPERTY AT 54 MONTEREY BLVD. AVAILABLE ON A MUTUALLY AGREEABLE DATE AND TIME WITHIN 20 DAYS OF THIS ORDER FOR DESTRUCTIVE TESTING WITHIN THE PARAMETERS LAID OUT IN THE CAL-BAY LETTER REPORT REFERENCED IN THE MOVING PAPERS AT PAGE 7 (KIM DECL., EXH. 4). IMMEDIATELY AFTER DESTRUCTIVE TESTING HAS CONCLUDED, THE PROPERTY MUST BE RESTORED TO ITS PRE-TESTING CONDITION. IF POST-TESTING RESTORATION IS IMPOSSIBLE IN A PARTICULAR AREA, THEN DESTRUCTIVE TESTING OF THAT AREA SHALL NOT OCCUR. PLAINTIFFS MAY ATTEND THE INSPECTION. SANCTIONS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT BOTH SIDE ACTED WITH SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: CHARLES GEERHART, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Inspection Of Property And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-11-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS BENEZRA TERGIS, AMIEE ZAWITZ'S MOTION TO COMPEL INSPECTION OF PROPERTY AND FOR SANCTIONS

Appearances: Paul A. Conroy of Aaron & Wilson LLP (415.438.7803) for defendant and responding party; David Garibaldi of Peretz & Associates (415.732.3777 x108) for plaintiffs and moving parties.

Counsel stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem.

This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Granted. Defendant shall make the property at 54 Monterey Blvd. available on a mutually agreeable date and time within 20 days of this order for destructive testing within the parameters laid out in the Cal-Bay letter report referenced in the moving papers at page 7 (Kim Decl., Exh. 4). Immediately after destructive testing has concluded, the property must be restored to its pre-testing condition. If post-testing restoration is impossible in a particular area, then destructive testing of that area shall not occur. Plaintiffs may attend the inspection. Sanctions denied. The court finds that both side acted with substantial justification. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Charles Geerhart, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (302/JPT)
